market.advice: Q2 2026 closed as the strongest quarter for equities since 2020 — and the internals argue this bull has more legs into Q3. VIX at 16.5 is falling from a 5-day average of 18.0; fear is draining from the market. Distribution days stand at just 1. Follow-through is confirmed. Breadth is moderate-healthy at 63% of stocks above their 50-day MA. Full position sizing is appropriate for clean, unextended setups. The dominant Q3 setup: Semiconductors (AMD/LRCX/TSM/ASML) are in synchronized Stage 2 breakouts — the AI infrastructure build-out is still in innings 2 or 3. Industrial Automation (ROK/GNRC) is the quiet leader — factory AI is the new trade capturing institutional rotation alongside big tech. Financials (C/AFL) are making new highs on a disinflating economy. Oil posting its steepest quarterly loss since 2020 is a deflationary gift — input costs falling as revenues rise is the margin expansion formula. Key risk: Iran nuclear talks and the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire situation remain wildcards; if either flares, tighten stops on extended names (PANW RSI 88.8, AMAT RSI 74.6, GLW RSI 74.9) first. For Q3: ride the Stage 2 leaders, add to dips in clean setups, and resist chasing high-RSI names.
